meaning: n.乖戾的(老)人,脾气坏的人,爱争吵的(老)家伙
examples: Indeed, whereas a young curmudgeon has no heart, an old idealist has no head.
The old curmudgeon was talking about the smothering effects of parental duty on creative lives.
Only a curmudgeon would deny the many benefits that our computers and electronic networks have brought us.
Call me a curmudgeon, but I detested those forced moments of gaiety and collegiality.
He was a self-described curmudgeon, often blunt, blasphemous and unbending yet extremely smart and seasoned in the news business.
